text: Lesson 28
How to Use the Playbook and Toolkit Together
textPreview: How to Use the Playbook and Toolkit Together

text: Staff Training Module
AI Playbook and Toolkit for Public Health Departments
textPreview: AI Playbook and Toolkit for Public Health Departments

alt: AI Playbook for Public Health logo

text:
Welcome participants to the How to Use the Playbook and Toolkit Together lesson.
Explain that this deck is designed for staff training, leadership orientation, governance preparation, or cross-program discussion.
Frame the topic in plain language: Read the play first to understand the decision and output, then use the linked tools during workshops or governance reviews.
Invite participants to think about where this topic already appears in their department's workflows, vendor tools, data systems, communications, or planning conversations.
AI PLAYBOOK AND TOOLKIT
Why this topic matters
Read the play first to understand the decision and output, then use the linked tools during workshops or governance reviews.
textPreview: Read the play first to understand the decision and output, then use the linked tools during workshops or governance reviews.

text: Public health value

The playbook creates the sequence and the toolkit creates the documentation. Using them together helps departments move from learning to readiness, governance, use case selection, funding, deployment, monitoring, and sustained oversight.
textPreview: The playbook creates the sequence and the toolkit creates the documentation. Using them together helps departments move from learning to ...

text: Guardrail
The tools support the plays; they should not become a disconnected paperwork exercise.
textPreview: The tools support the plays; they should not become a disconnected paperwork exercise.

text:
Use this slide in the How to Use the Playbook and Toolkit Together training module.
Use this slide to explain the practical public health reason for learning this topic before AI tools are approved or deployed.
Connect the discussion back to the playbook: this topic supports Plays 1, 2, 3, 11 and Tools 2, 7, 12, 41, 42.
Emphasize the core guardrail: The tools support the plays; they should not become a disconnected paperwork exercise.
Ask participants how this topic could affect their own program area, staff roles, community relationships, data, or public communications.
AI PLAYBOOK AND TOOLKIT
Learning objectives
By the end of this lesson, staff should be able to:
textPreview: By the end of this lesson, staff should be able to:

text:
• Explain How to Use the Playbook and Toolkit Together in plain language.
• Identify appropriate public health uses and limits.
• Describe human review, governance, equity, privacy, and monitoring expectations.
• Connect the topic to the related plays and tools.

text:
Use this slide in the How to Use the Playbook and Toolkit Together training module.
Walk through the objectives and connect each one to a practical implementation decision.
Connect the discussion back to the playbook: this topic supports Plays 1, 2, 3, 11 and Tools 2, 7, 12, 41, 42.
Explain that participants do not need technical expertise to use this material. The goal is enough shared understanding to participate in governance, readiness assessment, use case review, procurement, deployment, or monitoring.
AI PLAYBOOK AND TOOLKIT
Module overview

The playbook and toolkit are designed to work together. Plays define the decisions, sequence, participants, outputs, and governance gates. Tools help departments complete the work, document decisions, and create reusable artifacts.
textPreview: The playbook and toolkit are designed to work together. Plays define the decisions, sequence, participants, outputs, and governance gates...

AI PLAYBOOK AND TOOLKIT
What this means in practice
How to Use the Sequence

Start with vision and readiness so the department understands purpose, gaps, and constraints. Establish governance before formal use case selection, vendor engagement, pilots, or deployment. Use stakeholder engagement, workforce planning,.
textPreview: Start with vision and readiness so the department understands purpose, gaps, and constraints. Establish governance before formal use case...

text: How to Use the Tools

Open tools from the play page when the tool is needed for a decision or output. Save progress and update living tools as decisions change. Use completed tools as governance records, implementation artifacts, and evidence for leadership or.
textPreview: Open tools from the play page when the tool is needed for a decision or output. Save progress and update living tools as decisions change...

text: Governance Discipline

Do not skip early plays because a tool or vendor is available. Use assessment recommendations to customize the path based on readiness gaps and completed work. Treat the implementation plan, inventory, monitoring dashboard, and policy log as.

text:
Use this slide in the How to Use the Playbook and Toolkit Together training module.
Use this slide to turn the concept into practical behaviors, decisions, and safeguards.
Connect the discussion back to the playbook: this topic supports Plays 1, 2, 3, 11 and Tools 2, 7, 12, 41, 42.
Invite participants to identify where these practices would need to be documented in their own department.
Start with How to Use the Sequence, then compare it with the other practice areas on the slide.
AI PLAYBOOK AND TOOLKIT
Public health application
Use the topic to examine real workflows, not abstract technology.
textPreview: Use the topic to examine real workflows, not abstract technology.

text: Use Tool 7 during Play 2
Complete the readiness self-assessment before selecting pilots so gaps in governance, data, workforce, equity, and security are visible.
textPreview: Complete the readiness self-assessment before selecting pilots so gaps in governance, data, workforce, equity, and security are visible.

text: Use Tools 2 and 12 during Play 3
Pair the responsible AI policy template with the governance board charter to define authority, review standards, and decision rights.
textPreview: Pair the responsible AI policy template with the governance board charter to define authority, review standards, and decision rights.

text: Use Tools 41 and 42 before scaling
Use the pilot dashboard and scale decision framework to determine whether to scale, revise, pause, or retire a pilot.
textPreview: Use the pilot dashboard and scale decision framework to determine whether to scale, revise, pause, or retire a pilot.

text:
Use this slide in the How to Use the Playbook and Toolkit Together training module.
Use this slide to make the topic concrete through public health workflow examples.
Connect the discussion back to the playbook: this topic supports Plays 1, 2, 3, 11 and Tools 2, 7, 12, 41, 42.
For each example, ask: What problem is being solved? What data would be used? Who reviews the AI output? What decision remains human? What could go wrong?
AI PLAYBOOK AND TOOLKIT
Facilitated discussion and exercise
Discussion questions
Which plays are required based on the readiness assessment? Which tools document the decisions needed for those plays? Who owns each tool, output, deadline, and governance gate?
textPreview: Which plays are required based on the readiness assessment? Which tools document the decisions needed for those plays? Who owns each tool...

text: Training exercise
Take assessment results and convert them into a customized workplan with recommended plays, supporting tools, owners, deadlines, and governance milestones.

text:
Use this slide in the How to Use the Playbook and Toolkit Together training module.
Use this slide for a facilitated activity near the end of the lesson.
Connect the discussion back to the playbook: this topic supports Plays 1, 2, 3, 11 and Tools 2, 7, 12, 41, 42.
Give participants time to work individually or in small groups. Ask them to produce a practical artifact that can feed into readiness assessment, governance review, use case prioritization, or implementation planning.
Expected takeaways include: Customized play-and-tool workplan, Governance gate checklist, Completed tool package for leadership review.
